title: Lesson 3: (Kana) - Lesson 3: (Hiragana) "が, ぎ, ぐ, げ, ご"

intro_cards.title: Dakuten (゛)
intro_cards.body:
The two small marks (゛) at the top-right of a character are called dakuten (or 'tenten'). They voice the consonant:

か → が (ka → ga)
さ → ざ (sa → za)
た → だ (ta → da)
は → ば (ha → ba)

You'll learn each set as it comes. This lesson covers the か row: か → が, き → ぎ, く → ぐ, け → げ, こ → ご.
